questions.question_text: During a sale, 50% of the goods are sold at 21% profit, 40% of the remaining goods are sold at 36% profit and the still remaining goods are sold at a loss of 29%. If there is an overall profit of X%, then what is the value of X?
The largest four-digit number which when divided by 6, 14 and 7 leaves remainder 5 in each case is:
A shopkeeper marks an article 40% above its cost price. He then allows two successive discounts of 18% and 5% on the marked price. Find the profit percentage.
A trader buys a chair for ₹200, sells it at a 10% profit, and then the buyer sells it again at a 20% profit. What is the final selling price?
What is the digit y if the number 6y512 is exactly divisible by both 8 and 9?
Simplify: $15 + \frac{12}{\cot^2 A} - \frac{12}{\cos^2 A}$
The sum of two positive numbers is 35 and their product is 286. The positive difference between them is:
The average of five numbers 7, 11, 15, x, and 23 is 15. What is the value of x?
The average of 8 numbers is 45. If each number is decreased by 4, what will the new average be?
If a square's diagonal is extended by 6 units beyond a vertex, forming a triangle outside the square, what is the angle between this extension and the adjacent side of the square?
The sum of the ages of a daughter and her mother is 76 years. After 4 years, the age of the mother will be three times that of the daughter. What is the present age (in years) of the mother?

Two bags contain marbles in the ratio 5 : 7. If 12 marbles are transferred from the second bag to the first bag, the ratio of marbles in the two bags becomes 7 : 5. Find the original number of marbles in the second bag.
Girish scored 448 marks in an examination and was 64 marks short of 16% of maximum marks. In the same examination, his friend scored 472 marks. What is the percentage of marks scored by his friend?
A and B can complete a task in 20 days and 25 days, respectively. They started working together, but B left after 5 days. How many more days will A take to finish the remaining work?
The perimeter of a rectangle is 56 cm. Its length is 4 cm more than twice its breadth. Find the breadth.
A sum of ₹8,000 is invested at a compound interest rate of 12.5% per annum for 2 years, compounded annually. What is the compound interest earned?
If the compound interest on a sum for 2 years at 5% per annum is ₹102.50, what is the sum?

A solid cube is melted and half of its material is recast into a cuboid whose length, breadth, and height are in the ratio 2 : 1 : 1. The difference between the volume of the original cube and the cuboid formed is $250 \text{ cm}^3$. Find the total surface area of the cuboid.

A shopkeeper offers the following schemes on toys of the same marked price. (A) Successive discounts of 10% and 30% on any number of toys bought. (B) Successive discounts of 18%, 15% and 5% on any number of toys bought. (C) 15% discount on the first 3 toys and 10% discount on each toy thereon. (D) 2 toys free of cost on buying 8 toys. A customer wants to buy 5 toys. Which of the above schemes is the least beneficial to her?
Simplify $\left\{ \left( \frac{14}{24} \div \frac{9}{9} \right) \right\} + \left( \frac{3}{7} \times \frac{14}{8} \right) + \frac{9}{10} \div \frac{31}{24} \times \frac{24}{10}$
Simplify: $5.25 + 2.75 - \frac{3}{5} \times \frac{2}{5} \div \frac{10}{20}$
An altitude of $12 \text{ cm}$ is drawn to the base of an isosceles triangle. The perimeter of this triangle is $48 \text{ cm}$. Find the area of the triangle.
Find the third proportional to 6 and 12.
Three numbers in the ratio 2 : 5 : 7 are taken as the first, second, and third terms, respectively. If their fourth proportional is 140, find the middle number.
A bus covers 90 km in 3 hours. If the speed of the bus is increased by 5 kmph, find the time taken to cover a distance of 70 km?
The price of an article is increased by 25%. By what percentage should the increased price be reduced so that it becomes equal to the original price?
In a division sum, the divisor is 5 times the quotient and 4 times the remainder. If the remainder is 15, what is the dividend?

Two taps can fill a cistern in 4 hours and 10 hours, respectively. A third tap can empty it in 10 hours. How long (in hours) will it take to fill one-fourth of the empty cistern if all the taps are opened together?
If $x^2 \times x^3 = x^a$ and $\frac{x^b}{x^3} = x^1$, what is the value of $a+b$?
Ritu covers a distance of 40 km at 20 km/h and another distance at 40 km/h in 3 hours. Find the average speed for the whole journey.
Which session of the UN Climate Change Conference was held in Baku, Azerbaijan, in November 2024?
Through his renowned work Jnaneshwari, Sant Gyaneshwar rendered the Bhagavad Gita into which regional language?

What should come in place of '?' in the given series based on the English alphabetical order? QGT FJW IMZ LPC ?

Seven boxes S, R, T, U, V, W and X are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. V is kept neither at the top nor at the bottom. Only three boxes are kept between U and W. R is kept one of the positions between S and T. W is kept second from the bottom. U is immediately above V. How many boxes are kept between X and T?

Refer to the following letter series and answer the question that follows. Counting to be done from left to right only. (Left) S Z E C A O Q M D P U J L N X K K D (Right) How many such vowels are there, each of which is immediately preceded by a consonant and also immediately followed by a vowel?
In a certain code language, 'SFOR' is coded as '116' and 'WILA' is coded as '90'. What is the code for 'GMVC' in that given language?

Select the set in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following sets. (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into their constituent digits. E.g. $13 - 1$ operations in $13$ is as $12$, and not $(1-3)$ operations. Splitting of numbers is not allowed. Addition, subtraction, multiplication and then performing mathematical operations on $1$ and $3$ is not allowed.) $(5, 3, 19)$ $(6, 3, 9)$

In a certain code language: A - B means 'A is the husband of B'. A : B means 'A is the mother of B'. A + B means 'A is the brother of B'. A * B means 'A is the sister of B'. Based on the above, how is G related to R if 'G @ H + I - T - R'?

Seven boxes B, C, D, E, Q, R and S are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. Only two boxes are kept between B and S. Two boxes are kept between E and D. Only Q is kept above D. S is kept at some place below B but at some place above R. Which box is kept immediately above S?

Seven boxes C,D,E,F,G,H and P are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. Two boxes are kept between D and H. Only three boxes are kept between H and G. F is kept at one of the positions below C. Only H is kept between G and P. E is kept at one of the positions above G. How many boxes are kept between D and E?

In a certain code language, A @ B means 'A is the mother of B', A # B means 'A is the husband of B', A & B means 'A is the son of B' and A * B means 'A is the daughter of B'. If G * K & V @ P # W % J, then how is J related to G?

Seven people H, I, J, K, W, Y and Z are sitting in a row facing north. Only three people sit between H and W. K sits to the immediate right of W. No one sits to the right of I. Only two people sit between I and K. Z sits to the immediate right of J. How many people sit between Y and Z?
What will come in place of the question mark (?) in the following equation if '+' and '-' are interchanged and 'x' and '÷' are interchanged? $20 \div 4 - 3 + 75 \times 5 = ?$
If 2 is added to each odd digit and 2 is subtracted from each even digit in the number 7213548, which of the following digits will be third from the left in the new number thus formed?

Each letter in the word GUILDER is arranged in the English alphabetical order. How many letters are there in the English alphabetical order between the letter which is fourth from the left and the one which is second from the right in the new letter cluster thus formed?

Read the given statement(s) and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statement(s). Statements: All carrots are onions. All onions are peas. Conclusions (I): Some peas are carrots. Conclusions (II): Some onions are carrots.

He takes a final left turn, drives 11 km and stops at Point P. How far (shortest distance) and towards which direction should he drive in order to reach Point A again? (All turns on 90 degrees unless otherwise specified.)

Dn is not an immediate neighbour of Ej. How many people sit between An and Fm when counted from the left of Fm?
